results.title: ✨ Google AI Overview
results.snippet:
AI Overview
There is currently no permanent cure for diabetes, but the disease can be managed effectively, and Type 2 diabetes can often be put into remission. Breakthroughs in regenerative medicine and cell therapies are actively bringing researchers closer to a functional cure for Type 1 diabetes. 
The path to a "cure" depends on the type of diabetes:
Type 1 Diabetes (T1D)
Type 1 is an autoimmune disease where the body destroys its own insulin-producing beta cells. While a lifelong condition, significant medical breakthroughs are moving closer to a functional cure. 
Stem Cell-Derived Islet Transplants: Researchers—including biotech companies like Vertex Pharmaceuticals and major research centers like UCSF—are conducting clinical trials where lab-grown stem cells are transplanted into patients to replace lost pancreatic cells
